choiceresponse.label: Looking at the SHAP summary plot, you see that for thal_3, higher values (red points) tend to appear on the left side of the plot (negative SHAP values). Which conclusions are justified? (Select all that apply.)

checkboxgroup.choice: Higher thal_3 values tend to push predictions toward lower disease risk in this model.
Lower thal_3 values tend to increase the predicted probability of disease.
thal_3 has no effect on the model’s predictions.
The direction of the effect of thal_3 is consistent across all patients.
The plot reflects model behavior, not clinical causality.

div.p: SHAP values encode both magnitude and direction of a feature’s effect on the model output. The SHAP summary plot shows that higher values of thal_3 (the red points) tend to have negative SHAP values, meaning they usually lower the model’s predicted risk of disease. Conversely, lower values of thal_3 (the blue points) tend to have positive SHAP values, which push the prediction toward higher risk. However, this describes how the model behaves, not causal relationships, and individual patients may still deviate from the overall trend.
